data.title: Informatica ticks lower amid report on Salesforce deal close timing
data.description:
Informatica (INFA) edged lower by 0.3% after a report about the timing of Salesforce's $8 billion planned acquisition. 

There appears to be some uncertainty whether the Informatica (INFA) deal could close this year even though it has cleared several regulatory hurdles, according to a CTFN report on Thursday, which cited industry sources. 

One source familiar with Salesforce's data teams told the publication that coordination between Salesforce and Informatica (INFA) is still in the "early stages." 

Salesforce and Informatica didn't respond to CTFN's requests for comment.

Salesforce (CRM) is scheduled to report Q3 results on Dec. 3 and investors are hoping to find out more about the Informatica deal close timing.

In late May, Salesforce (CRM) announced a deal to pay $25 per share  in cash for enterprise cloud software company Informatica (INFA).

data.title: GoHealth outlines disciplined strategy and industry consolidation focus amid Medicare Advantage market shifts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: GoHealth (GOCO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Vijay Kotte outlined a disciplined and cautious approach in response to changes in the Medicare Advantage market, emphasizing, "We pulled back intentionally" and prioritized "quality over quantity; retention over short-term submissions; and cash preservation and strategic flexibility." Kotte explained that health plans are now focusing on member retention, stable profiles, and unit economics rather than broad expansion.
  • The company shifted significant capacity into GoHealth Protect during the SEP and adjusted marketing and compensation strategies to reinforce objective guidance, even confirming existing plans for consumers when appropriate. Kotte stated, "We have significantly reduced overhead while continuing to invest in AI and automation that improve agent effectiveness, consumer experience and member retention."
  • GoHealth maintains a focus on its leadership position in special needs plans, with Kotte noting, "Total available nonspecial needs plan products declined for 2026, while special needs plan options increased. This reflects a clear industry priority, targeted growth where value and continuity of care are highest."
  • The CEO emphasized the company’s readiness to lead integration in a fragmented broker landscape, mentioning, "With a stronger balance sheet, lender support and a refreshed Board, we believe that we are positioned to lead integration where it creates value, reducing duplicative costs, improving back book cash flow, stabilizing membership retention and enhancing the consumer experience."
  • Kotte reiterated, "Our pullback is intentional. We prioritize long-term value creation rather than near-term enrollment volume."

Outlook

  • Management did not provide specific revenue or EPS guidance for upcoming quarters. However, Kotte stated, "We believe GoHealth is positioned to return to revenue growth consistent with prior years when the market rationalizes, but with a meaningfully stronger margin and cash profile."
  • The company signaled continued investment in technology, AI, and automation to position itself for a market rebound and to support potential industry consolidation.
  • Compared with last quarter, the strategic focus shifted further to retention and stability, with an explicit de-emphasis on new enrollment volume.

Financial Results

  • GoHealth reported "approximately $32 million of cash at the end of the third quarter" (CEO Vijay Kotte). Kotte also noted access to a $40 million super priority facility for additional liquidity.
  • There was no explicit mention of revenue or EPS figures for the quarter in the transcript.
  • The company highlighted its "significantly reduced overhead" and ongoing investment in technology to maintain efficiency and readiness for future ramp-up.
  • No one-time items or special charges were cited in the remarks.

Q&A

  • Robert McGuire, Granite Research, LLC: Asked about the Medicare Advantage growth trajectory and catalysts for market reacceleration. CEO Kotte explained, "Over 12 to 24 months, we do believe that the health plans according to their own projections, will have stabilized our cost structures, will be refocused in which geographies and which products make most sense to them."
  • McGuire followed up on GoHealth Protect growth and product seasonality. Kotte responded that GoHealth Protect and Medicare Advantage are "very complementary," and the company will continue to oscillate focus based on market conditions.
  • Patrick McCann, NOBLE Capital Markets: Asked about the rationale for pulling back on MA and potential consequences. Kotte said, "If we're wrong, we've just got to re-ramp and get going. But if we're right...the worst thing we could have done was to deploy a bunch of capital into this market."
  • McCann queried industry consolidation. Kotte stated, "We believe that a large volume of brokers in the industry...introduces additional churn by their natural existence. We do believe there's duplicative expense on the fixed cost side...if you were to be able to combine, you could take the power of a strong consumer base..."
  • Benjamin Hendrix, RBC: Queried about carrier attitudes and GoHealth’s ability to ramp up sales. Kotte explained, "Nobody wants broad-based growth. Everybody wants even more than we've ever seen, very specific targeted growth...we're maintaining that capacity, maintaining that capability, continuing to invest in the AI and automation tools that standardize the process."
  • James Sidoti, Sidoti & Company, LLC: Asked about cash burn and liquidity. Kotte reported "approximately $32 million of cash at the end of the third quarter" and access to the $40 million facility, stating, "We think it maintains our capabilities, our stability and still allows us to make investments."
  • David Storms, Stonegate: Inquired about retention logistics and special needs plans. Kotte described targeted agent compensation and technology investments, noting early indications of "our retention rate is better than the field" and detailed GoHealth's proprietary tools for special needs plan enrollment.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly cautious tone, focusing on the company’s ability to navigate market uncertainty, cash management, and readiness for industry consolidation. Questions pressed for details on ramp-up capability and strategic rationale behind the MA pullback.
  • Management’s tone was confident and disciplined during prepared remarks, repeatedly emphasizing a deliberate and data-driven strategy: "We chose the disciplined path." In Q&A, Kotte’s responses were measured, acknowledging risks but stressing flexibility and preparedness: "If we're wrong, we've just got to re-ramp and get going."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remains confident but even more focused on discipline, risk management, and long-term positioning. Analysts’ tone remains probing but not overtly negative.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a pronounced shift to prioritizing member retention and quality over growth, whereas last quarter’s focus was on capital structure improvements and readiness for the AEP.
  • Strategic focus has moved from preparing for operational flexibility and M&A to emphasizing technological investment, retention, and specialization in special needs plans.
  • Analysts’ attention shifted from capital restructuring and M&A readiness to operational execution, liquidity, and GoHealth’s playbook for market stabilization.
  • Management’s cautious optimism is more pronounced, with greater emphasis on discipline and risk avoidance. The tone remains confident but is less focused on immediate growth and more on preservation and future optionality.

Risks and Concerns

  • CEO Kotte highlighted industry-wide challenges: "Health plans tighten plan economics, reduced prefunded marketing and adjusted broker compensation. In some cases, the most consumer preferred plans were made non-commissionable or were suppressed."
  • Risks cited include market uncertainty, reduced commissionable products, and the potential for lower renewal rates, which could impact cash-on-cash returns.
  • Management’s strategy to mitigate these risks includes reducing overhead, preserving cash, investing in technology, and maintaining readiness for rapid operational ramp-up.
  • Analysts voiced concerns about liquidity, cash burn, and the company’s ability to re-scale operations if market dynamics shift unexpectedly.

Final Takeaway

GoHealth management stressed that its cautious, data-driven approach is designed to preserve core capabilities, prioritize long-term value, and maintain flexibility amid a challenging Medicare Advantage landscape. The company remains optimistic about future growth opportunities, especially in special needs plans, and intends to leverage its technology and strong balance sheet to capitalize on industry consolidation when the market stabilizes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. stocks sink as post-shutdown optimism fades, rate cut odds fall below 50%
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes were significantly lower on Thursday as heightened market sentiment due to the end of the U.S. government shutdown dissipated, and focus was turned to the economy.

Odds of a December 25-basis-point interest rate cut have fallen to 47.6%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -1.7% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2.5%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.6%.

Some of the mega-caps leading the decline within the S&P 500 ((SP500)) include Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Tesla (TSLA), and Palantir (PLTR).

The U.S. House of Representatives on Wednesday passed the stopgap spending bill that will end the longest U.S. government shutdown in history, which lasted 43 days. With it, President Donald Trump signed into law a federal spending package.

With the shutdown now over for the time being, investors are hoping that delayed economic reports will soon start to come. However, two major economic reports on jobs and inflation for October may never be released even as the government shutdown ends, according to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt.

“The gears of the government should be working again soon, and while that is a relief for markets and the economy, there is still plenty of uncertainty, particularly around the missed inflation and jobs data and how these fronts have been faring. While we have always expected that many of the data points missed during the shutdown will remain dark, there are questions about what the inflation and jobs data will look like once these reports come back online,” said Carol Schleif, chief market strategist at BMO Private Wealth.

“We would not be surprised to see some market chop over the coming weeks as the government gears and economic data presses get turning again. The data blackout has made the Federal Reserve's job difficult, but we still expect them to cut interest rates again in December,” Schleif added.

In addition, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said on Thursday that the Fed should remain “somewhat restrictive” due to softening in the labor market, and inflation still above the Fed's 2% target.

On the earnings side, The Walt Disney Company (DIS) was last -8% after the company published its FQ4 earnings report. Revenue dipped 0.5% year-over-year as the Entertainment segment saw revenue decline 6%.

On the other hand, Cisco (CSCO) shares were +4.1% after the networking equipment giant reported fiscal first-quarter results that were above Wall Street's estimates and raised its full-year forecast. The company's stock is near its all-time high.

“Earnings so far have been better than expected, with double-digit growth rates and solid participation across most economic sectors. Better yet, the bottom line is coming in well ahead of strong revenue growth, indicating intense focus on extracting margin improvement,” said Schleif.

“It's becoming clear that earnings expectations have been too low ever since they were cut in the aftermath of Liberation Day in April. That has also helped stocks clear a lower bar and helped to propel stocks to record heights,” she added.

On crypto, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped below $100,000 for the first time since May.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.11%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.60%. A $25B, 30-Year Bond Auction tailed.

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day.

Wall Street's major averages ended mixed on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones reaching a new record high close, while Nasdaq ended in the red, as several big tech stocks lagged.

More on the markets:

data.title: What does the future look like for Wall Street?
data.description:
Wall Street's broader market averages are under heavy pressure on Thursday afternoon with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dropping over 2% and both the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow (DJI) both lower by 1.5%.

The downward shift has some investors panicked and others ready for opportunities. The question becomes where do markets go from here?

Outlined below are the opinions of some Seeking Alpha analysts and where they see the future heading.

The Bulls 

SA analyst Multiplo Invest stated: “Expect the S&P 500 to end the year above 7000, with attractive risk-return and continued support from positive macroeconomic news,” in What If The End Of The Shutdown Is The Start Of A Rally?

SA analyst ANG Traders echoed: “The current bull market is not near its end, with several years likely remaining before reaching typical cycle length and valuations,” in That Which Is Obvious...Is Obviously Wrong.

The Bears

SA analyst Hawkinvest said: “Stock market valuations appear stretched as economic warning signs intensify, with parallels to the Great Financial Crisis emerging in key data,” in 3 Ominous Charts Are Pointing To Another Great Financial Crisis. 

SA analyst Agar Capital expressed: “Market structure remains fragile, with narrow leadership; I favor reduced beta and broad diversification until stronger catalysts emerge or a correction occurs… US job market data signals weakness, with over 1 million layoffs announced this year and AI-driven efficiency contributing to job cuts,” in The Market Is Cracking Quietly: Breadth, Job Cuts, And A Fragile Recovery. 

Additional Information

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the broader market’s they can always shift their attention towards exchange traded funds: 

Dow ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW).

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX). 

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QLD), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Global Water Resources outlines $4.3M rate increase proposal and expands Tucson integration as growth drivers
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Global Water Resources, Inc. (GWRS) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Ron Fleming, Chairman, CEO & President, reported the closing of the Tucson acquisition, which included 7 public water systems and approximately 2,200 connections, adding about $7.7 million in rate base at a multiple of 1.05x. Fleming stated, "This is beyond an attractive price that is immediately accretive from a share price perspective, considering our peer group trades by our estimates between 1.5x to 2x rate base." The systems are expected to generate $1.5 million in annual revenue until consolidation into the Saguaro rate division.
  • Fleming highlighted the enactment of Arizona's Ag-to-Urban water legislation, which "will result in many benefits that will be applicable for Global Water in our service areas, improving offer for sustainability, while creating a new ground water supply to support additional growth."
  • Full funding of the Highway 347 expansion was approved, with construction expected to begin as early as fiscal year 2026. Fleming noted the City of Maricopa's population growth, with 7.4% in 2024 compared to 7.1% in 2023, and emphasized its relevance to consumption and revenue growth.
  • Total active service connections increased 6.6% to 68,130 as of September 30, 2025. Infrastructure investment year-to-date reached $49.6 million, with a focus on post-test year projects for the Santa Cruz Water Company and Palo Verde Utility Company.
  • Discussing organic growth, Fleming noted a pullback in permit activity, with Phoenix MSA Q3 building permits down 29% and Maricopa down 20% year-over-year. He asserted, "We believe this is temporary and as these things continue to cool, there were very strong drivers for our normal growth rate to continue or even pick up."
  • Fleming detailed a proposed $4.3 million annual rate increase under consideration at the Arizona Corporation Commission (ACC).
  • Michael Liebman, Senior VP, CFO & Corporate Secretary, stated, "Total revenue for the third quarter of 2025 was $15.5 million, which was up $1.2 million or 8.4% compared to Q3 2024."
  • Liebman added, "Net income for Q3 2025 was $1.7 million or $0.06 per diluted share as compared to $2.9 million or $0.12 per diluted share in Q3 2024."
  • Christopher Krygier, Chief Operating Officer, described the rate case process as "in the middle innings of the process," with expectations to complete in mid-2026.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated expectations to complete the rate case by mid-2026, with Fleming confirming, "we still expect a fair outcome in mid-2026."
  • The proposed $4.3 million annual rate increase is currently under ACC consideration, with a median bill increase of less than 10% for the typical residential customer, according to Krygier.
  • No comparison to analysts' estimates is provided as sufficient data was not available.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q3 2025 was $15.5 million, up $1.2 million compared to Q3 2024.
  • Year-to-date revenue reached $42.2 million, an increase of $2.8 million.
  • Operating expenses for Q3 were $12.6 million, up $2.3 million year-over-year, primarily due to personnel costs, integration of acquired systems, and storm-related expenses.
  • Net income decreased to $1.7 million from $2.9 million in Q3 2024, while adjusted EBITDA was $7.8 million compared to $8.2 million in the prior year.
  • Infrastructure investments year-to-date totaled $49.6 million.

Q&A

  • Gerard Sweeney, ROTH Capital Partners: Questioned timing of the rate case and whether July 1 remains a feasible date for rate changes. Michael Liebman confirmed, "that timing puts us to where we expect the rates to change by the middle of the year. So July 1 in the back half of the year with the new rates in place."
  • Sweeney asked about the Ag-to-Urban program's impact and mechanics. Ron Fleming explained, "We don't have to buy it. We don't lease it. The land owner has the right to pump water... So it's kind of a win-win-win because the farming goes away and then we just convert a portion of those historic groundwater pumping rights to a new municipal water supply."
  • Sweeney inquired about the Highway 347 expansion's potential to drive Maricopa growth. Fleming responded, "It's going to allow us to keep booming. So we're pretty excited about it."
  • Matvey Tayts, Freedom Broker: Asked about the ACC proposal being 50% below the company's request. Krygier stated, "we're kind of still in the process... So that's part of it. And then obviously, they're still back and forth asking for additional information related to other investments..."
  • Tayts questioned the absence of the $212.5 million expected rate base in the new report. Liebman responded, "that number has actually -- it's come down a little bit from that $212 million, but materially -- that's still correct. And that's a number -- we just don't report that stuff in our quarterly financials."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly pressing, focusing on clarity for rate case timing, legislative impacts, and financial expectations, with questions seeking granular detail on regulatory and growth initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, emphasizing long-term growth drivers and accretive acquisitions. Fleming stated, "Despite many headwinds, we will continue to execute our growth plan."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone remained consistent, while management’s confidence was steady, continuing to highlight the company’s resilience and growth strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The proposed annual rate increase decreased to $4.3 million from $6.5 million noted in the previous quarter, reflecting updated regulatory filings.
  • Revenue and adjusted EBITDA increased compared to Q2, but net income for Q3 decreased compared to the prior year’s Q3.
  • Operational focus shifted further toward integrating the Tucson acquisition and progressing the rate case.
  • Analysts’ focus on the timing and scale of rate changes and the regulatory process remained consistent, while management continued to emphasize strategic infrastructure investments and legislative benefits.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged headwinds from inflation and cost increases, particularly as new rates are needed to address a five-year period since the last rate case.
  • Permit activity in core markets showed a pronounced slowdown, with management attributing this to macroeconomic uncertainty and expressing belief in future recovery.
  • Regulatory uncertainty remains around the ACC's rate case process and final outcomes, with company representatives describing the process as ongoing and subject to further negotiation and review.

Final Takeaway

Global Water Resources, Inc. highlighted progress in integrating new acquisitions, advancing a significant rate case with a $4.3 million increase proposal, and leveraging legislative and infrastructure developments to support long-term growth. Management remains committed to executing its growth plan and adapting to regulatory and market dynamics as it positions the company for continued expansion in Arizona’s fastest-growing communities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Fiserv settles suit over alleged breaches of USPS Move Update rules
data.description:
Fiserv (FISV) has reached a deal to settle and fully resolve a lawsuit alleging non-compliance with U.S. Postal Service ("USPS") Move Update regulations, it said on Thursday.

The case, titled United States ex rel. Deborah Lynn Getchman v. Fiserv Solutions, LLC and Fi, involved historical operations of the company's output solutions business.

The suit was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Missouri.

In recent years, Fiserv (FISV) has implemented a firm-wide compliance framework that "incorporates the strongest elements of previous programs to promote consistency and adherence to USPS expectations," it said.

Fiserv (FISV) fully cooperated with the USPS and the U.S. Department of Justice throughout the probe.

FISV shares were beaten down at the end of last month, when the company revamped its leadership, refreshed its board, slashed its full-year earnings guidance, and launched a new action plan after its Q3 earnings missed the mark. The selloff extended after a media report disclosed that the Fiserv's small-merchant customers were getting fed up with the extra fees attached to the Clover payment systems.

FISV shares edged up 0.9% in midafternoon trading, but down 47% M/M and 68% Y/Y.

data.title: Fastenal cut to Sell equivalent at Wolfe, seeing tariff-related inflation ahead
data.description:
Fastenal (FAST) trades roughly flat in choppy trading Thursday, as Wolfe Research downgraded shares to Underperform from Peer Perform with a $43 price target, anticipating an impact from tariff-related inflation.

Fastenal (FAST) shares have been lagging for the past few months following weaker than expected monthly sales since August and price realization, however valuation remains at a significant premium to peers at 34x and vulnerable to further compression risk, Wolfe analyst Nigel Coe wrote.

"We now see inflationary pressures beginning to weigh on gross margin performance," Coe wrote. "FAST's multiple has been normalizing lower, but we see risk of further compression since relative valuation remains challenging."

Coe sees gross margin pressure building, as inflationary forces increasingly flow through, noting it takes roughly six months for input cost changes to be reflected in Fastenal's (FAST) cost of goods sold because of FIFO inventory and slow turns, which means the full measure of tariff and related inflation has yet to be seen, with headwinds building into Q4 2025 and Q1 2026.

The analyst acknowledges potential for some inflation relief if IEEPA tariffs are repealed by the U.S. Supreme Court, but the analyst believes Fastenal (FAST) is more exposed to Section 232 tariffs and thus less advantaged than less steel-intensive competitors.

Coe said he is skeptical of scope for positive earnings surprises for Fastenal (FAST) if daily sales growth is on a (modestly) decelerating path, and sees a period of more elevated gross margin pressure.

data.title: Odds of December Fed rate cut sink amid hawkish commentary, market selloff
data.description:
Odds of another quarter-point interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve next month slumped on Thursday, amid some hawkish comments from policymakers and a Wall Street selloff driven by a slide in tech stocks.

According to the CME FedWatch tool, traders now see a 49.6% chance of a 25 basis point rate cut in December, compared to 62.9% a day ago, 69.6% a week ago, and a whopping 95.5% a month ago. 

Amid a paucity of economic data due to the U.S. government shutdown (which finally ended on Wednesday) and concerns of overstretched valuations in mega-cap tech stocks, traders have taken some money off the table since last week, sending the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) lower. Meanwhile, Fedspeak has leaned towards the hawkish side.

Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack on Thursday said the Fed had to "remain somewhat restrictive" to bring inflation down at a time when both mandates of the central bank - price stability and stable employment - have been under pressure. 

Hammack's remarks come a day after Boston Fed President Susan Collins said she was cautious about further rate cuts due to the lack of economic indicators. Also on Wednesday, Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic said inflation continued to pose the most urgent risk to economic stability.  

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500 (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: The iconic MGM Grand in Las Vegas completes its remodel
data.description:
MGM Resorts International (MGM) announced on Thursday that it completed a $300 million redesigned of the iconic MGM Grand Hotel & Casino in Las Vegas. 

The renovation included work on all 3,969 rooms and suites in its main tower. The project also introduced 111 new suites, bringing the total to 753. Each room now features spa-style bathrooms with glass-enclosed showers, integrated illuminated closets, and blackout drapery for better rest. Bright materials and wall-mounted Smart TVs were noted to create a sleek, open atmosphere, while upgraded minibars include guest-use refrigerators. The new decor was said to mix playful artwork, bold patterns, and rich finishes, with modern touches like USB-C charging stations and LED art installations throughout the tower.

The redesign coincides with MGM Grand’s expanding entertainment lineup, including the debut of Netflix Bites, the Palm Tree Beach Club by DJ Kygo’s Palm Tree Crew, The Friends Experience, and FREQNCY, a new Jabbawockeez show. 

Opened in 1993, MGM Grand Hotel & Casino was built on the site of the former Marina Hotel and Tropicana Country Club, quickly becoming one of the largest hotels in the world. Originally themed around old Hollywood glamour, the property featured the Emerald City motif from The Wizard of Oz and housed the MGM Grand Adventures Theme Park. Over the years, the resort has undergone several reimaginings to align with evolving guest preferences and the changing Las Vegas landscape. The property's entertainment legacy has been shaped by headliners like David Copperfield, Cirque du Soleil’s KÀ, and world-class boxing events.

data.title: Booking Holdings earns upgrade at Wedbush on bullish Q3 results, attractive valuation
data.description:
Booking Holdings’ (BKNG) recent quarterly results underscored the company’s ability to gain share within alternative lodging while engaging cost efficiencies, both of which enable the online travel agency ((OTA)) to reinvest in growth levers toward longer-term targets.

“Global travel demand was healthy relative to expectations this quarter, and Booking remains the best-positioned OTA, in our view, giving the company’s global scale and diversification, strong liquidity and free cash flow conversion,” said Wedbush analyst Scott Devitt, who upgraded the stock to Outperform from Neutral and set a $6,000 price target, representing 17% upside from Wednesday’s closing price.

The company reported strong Q3 results across key metrics and set healthy Q4 guidance versus street expectations. While the results gave Booking (BKNG) shares a lift, the stock is still down 10% from August and are currently trading ~17.6x Wedbush’s 2027 GAAP EPS estimates, near the low end of its historical range over the last two years, adding to Devitt’s Outperform thesis.

Looking ahead, Devitt sees gross bookings growing 11.5% year-over-year, ~100 basis points from his prior estimate, while room night growth is seen rising ~90 basis points to +7.2% year-over-year.

Further, Devitt expects Booking (BKNG) to deliver adjusted EBITDA of $9.8B with 180 basis points of margin expansion to 36.8%.

After gaining 4.5% over the previous four days, shares have returned 0.20% on Thursday.
